edit: Based on this thought, capabilities are organization-specific and represent the complex interactions among the organizational resources (Panda & Rath, 2021b; Wang and Ahmed, 2007).

what is distinction between resources and capabilities  based on resource based view ?

According to the resource-based view (RBV), resources and capabilities are distinct concepts. 

Resources refer to the tangible and intangible assets that an organization possesses, such as financial capital, physical assets, technology, intellectual property, and human resources. These resources provide the foundation for an organization's competitive advantage.

On the other hand, capabilities refer to an organization's ability to effectively and efficiently utilize its resources to achieve desired outcomes. Capabilities are the unique combinations and integration of resources, knowledge, skills, and processes that enable an organization to perform certain activities and tasks better than its competitors.

In essence, while resources are the inputs that an organization possesses, capabilities are the dynamic and evolving abilities that stem from and leverage these resources to create competitive advantages. Resources alone are not sufficient; the capabilities derived from combining and leveraging resources effectively are what give organizations a sustained competitive edge.

revise: Based on this notion, capabilities are organization-specific and represent the unique combinations and integration of the organizational resources that enable an organization to perform ahead of its competitors

Revised: Building on this understanding, capabilities are specific to each organization and encompass the distinctive combinations and integration of organizational resources that empower the organization to outperform its competitors.

summarize : Based on this notion, capabilities are organization-specific and represent the unique combinations and integration of the organizational resources that enable an organization to perform ahead of its competitors in order to gain competitive advantage, while resources provide the foundation for an organization’s competitive advantage (Panda & Rath, 2021b; Wang and Ahmed, 2007).

In summary, capabilities, according to this perspective, are specific to each organization and involve the unique integration and combination of its resources. These capabilities enable an organization to outperform its competitors and gain a competitive advantage. Resources, on the other hand, serve as the foundation for an organization's competitive advantage (Panda & Rath, 2021b; Wang and Ahmed, 2007).
revise:  it has gained strategic importance through providing a foundation to develop IT capabilities
Revised: It has acquired strategic significance by serving as a foundation for the development of IT capabilities.

revise: To address this view, the study employs the RBV and develops an empirical model to explore the mechanisms provided by IT capabilities to mediate the affect of  physical IT infrastructure on the digital transformation efforts in hospitals.

Revised: In response to this perspective, the study utilizes the RBV and constructs an empirical model to examine how IT capabilities mediate the impact of physical IT infrastructure on the digital transformation efforts within hospitals.

edit: Based on  Zhang & Tansuhaj's (2007) study, we propose a higher-level construct of IT capabilities that encompasses three dimensions: IT human capability, IT relationship capability, and IT architecture capability. In contrast, physical IT infrastructure is considered as IT resources.

In contrast, differentiating between resources and capabilities, physical IT infrastructure is considered as IT resources.

improve: Our study has made a significant contribution to the understanding of the role of physical IT infrastructure in hospitals’ digital transformation efforts. Our findings indicated that the physical IT infrastructure intricately affect strategic alignment and digital transformation efforts. In this process, three important IT capabilities, including IT human capability, IT relationship capability and IT relationship capability serve as mechanisms to fully mediate the influence of physical IT infrastructure on strategic alignment and digital transformation. Although the physical IT infrastructure lacks a direct effect, it has acquired strategic importance by serving as a foundation for the development of the IT capabilities that facilitate strategic alignment and drive digital transformation in hospitals. Thus, the results challenged the view that the physical IT infrastructure is a commodity with rise of digital transformation efforts. Furthermore, our study has emphasized the crucial role of strategic alignment as a facilitator of digital transformation in hospitals. As hospitals continue to integrate new digital technologies into their business processes, finding the right fit between these technologies and hospital strategies becomes increasingly complex. So Strategic alignment has become a crucial prerequisite for digital transformation effort, as it enables hospitals to effectively integrate new technologies with their goals and priorities.

Revised: Our study has significantly contributed to the understanding of the role played by physical IT infrastructure in the digital transformation efforts of hospitals. Our findings highlight the intricate impact of physical IT infrastructure on strategic alignment and digital transformation. The study identifies three key IT capabilities - IT human capability, IT relationship capability, and IT architecture capability - as essential mechanisms that fully mediate the influence of physical IT infrastructure on strategic alignment and digital transformation. Despite lacking a direct effect, physical IT infrastructure has gained strategic importance by providing a foundation for the development of IT capabilities that foster strategic alignment and drive digital transformation in hospitals. This challenges the notion that physical IT infrastructure is merely a commodity in the era of digital transformation. Furthermore, our study highlights the critical role of strategic alignment as a facilitator of digital transformation in hospitals. With the integration of new digital technologies into hospital operations, achieving alignment between these technologies and organizational strategies becomes increasingly complex. Strategic alignment thus becomes a vital prerequisite for successful digital transformation efforts as it enables hospitals to effectively integrate new technologies within their goals and priorities.

discuses more : Our study has significantly contributed to the understanding of the role played by physical IT infrastructure in the digital transformation efforts of hospitals. Our findings indicated the indirect impact of physical IT infrastructure on strategic alignment and digital transformation. The study identifies three key IT capabilities - IT human capability, IT relationship capability, and IT architecture capability - as essential mechanisms that fully mediate the influence of physical IT infrastructure on strategic alignment and digital transformation. Despite lacking a direct effect, physical IT infrastructure has gained strategic importance by providing a foundation for the development of IT capabilities that foster strategic alignment and drive digital transformation in hospitals. This challenges the notion that physical IT infrastructure is merely a commodity with rise of digital transformation efforts. Furthermore, our study highlights the critical role of strategic alignment as a facilitator of digital transformation in hospitals. With the integration of new digital technologies into hospital operations, achieving alignment between these technologies and organizational strategies becomes increasingly complex. Strategic alignment thus becomes a vital prerequisite for digital transformation efforts as it enables hospitals to effectively integrate new technologies within their goals and priorities.

Our study has made a significant contribution to enhancing the understanding of the role played by physical IT infrastructure in hospitals' digital transformation endeavors. It unveiled the indirect impact of physical IT infrastructure on strategic alignment and digital transformation. By identifying three pivotal IT capabilities - IT human capability, IT relationship capability, and IT architecture capability - as crucial mediating mechanisms, our research shed light on the intricate relationship between physical IT infrastructure and strategic alignment as well as digital transformation. Contrary to the perception that physical IT infrastructure is merely a commodity amidst the rise of digital transformation efforts, our findings underline its strategic importance. Physical IT infrastructure serves as a foundation for the development of IT capabilities that not only foster strategic alignment but also drive the overall digital transformation of hospitals. 

Furthermore, our study emphasizes the critical role of strategic alignment as a facilitator of successful digital transformation in hospitals. As hospitals integrate new digital technologies into their core business processes, the complexity of aligning these technologies with organizational strategies increases significantly. Hence, achieving strategic alignment becomes pivotal for effective digital transformation efforts. It allows hospitals to seamlessly integrate new technologies within their existing goals and priorities, ensuring the smooth progression of the digital transformation journey. Ultimately, our research highlights the interconnected nature of physical IT infrastructure, strategic alignment, and successful digital transformation in the healthcare industry.
edit: This challenges the notion that physical IT infrastructure is merely a commodity with the rise of digital transformation efforts.
Revised: These findings challenge the prevailing notion that physical IT infrastructure is merely considered a commodity in the context of emerging digital transformation efforts.
